Defendant Wyndham Vacation Ownership Inc, Trendwest Resorts Inc Notice Of Motion For Orders 1) Compelling Depositions Of Plaintiff'S Experts Prior To May 14, 2012 Trial And 2) Granting Defendants Leave To Depose Experts After The Statutory Close Of Expert Discovery
Off Calendar May-07-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Erin M. Connell, Esq., of Orrick Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP, Tel. 415.773.5969, appearing for defendant and moving party.
Randall E. Strauss, Esq., of Gwilliam Ivary Chiosso Cavalli & Brewer, Tel. 510.832.5411, appearing for plaintiff and opposing party.

The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Wyndham Vacation Ownership Inc. and Trendwest Resorts Inc.'s Motion For Orders 1) Compelling Depositions Of Plaintiff'S Experts Prior To May 14, 2012 Trial And 2) Granting Defendants Leave To Depose Experts After The Statutory Close Of Expert Discovery is Denied. Plaintiff was not obligated to produce the witnesses for deposition because Defendants did not serve a new expert witness demand after the Court of Appeal remanded the case. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. There is no indication that plaintiff had cited or otherwise informed defendants of Hirano, the controlling authority on this technical point of procedure, prior to defendants' filing of the motion or that, had defendants known about Hirano, they would have filed their motion. Moreover, all plaintiff needed to do to oppose the motion was to cite Hirano, which should cost far less than $7200 to do. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: Loretta Najera CSR # 5153
